duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Liquidation reward calculated using futureValue in borrow token decimals (6 for USDC) but compared/added to collateral amounts in collateral token decimals (18 for WETH), making rewards ~10^12 times too small and destroying liquidation incentives
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same Math.mulDivUp(debtPosition.futureValue, liquidationRewardPercent, PERCENT) unit mismatch in Liquidate.sol executeLiquidate. ECON-H001 selected for most comprehensive attack economics and victim impact analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Multicall borrowAToken cap validation uses balanceOf(address(this)) instead of totalSupply(), completely bypassing the protocol's borrow token deposit cap during multicall batches
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same balanceOf(address(this)) vs totalSupply() mismatch in Multicall.sol lines 19-20 and CapsLibrary.validateBorrowATokenIncreaseLteDebtTokenDecrease. ECON-H002 selected for CRITICAL rating and comprehensive explanation of the bypass mechanism.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: LiquidateWithReplacement transfers liquidatorProfitBorrowToken to feeRecipient instead of msg.sender (the keeper/liquidator), removing keeper economic incentive
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same fund routing error at LiquidateWithReplacement.sol line 120-121 where transferFrom sends to feeRecipient instead of msg.sender. TECH-H003 selected for clearest technical description with precise line references and clear impact chain.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Swap fee asymmetry between getCashAmountOut (fee on gross maxCashAmountOut) and getCreditAmountIn (fee on net cashAmountOut) allows systematic fee reduction via exactAmountIn=false path
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same fee base inconsistency in AccountingLibrary.sol between getCashAmountOut and getCreditAmountIn. ECON2-H014 selected for HIGH severity rating, detailed economics, and comprehensive code analysis.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: UpdateConfig crLiquidation check enforces strict decrease only (params.value >= current reverts), creating a one-way ratchet that prevents admin from increasing liquidation threshold
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same comparison error at UpdateConfig.sol line 82-85 where >= prevents any increase of crLiquidation. ECON-H007 selected for HIGH severity and comprehensive impact analysis on protocol risk management.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: SellCreditMarket event emits params.tenor for the dueDate field instead of the actual due date timestamp, corrupting off-chain indexing
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same event emission error in SellCreditMarket.executeSellCreditMarket where params.tenor is passed twice. TECH-H006 selected for clearest description with precise line references. Note: TECH2-H005 ID also covers a distinct Self-Liquidation issue (retained separately).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Compensate with RESERVED_ID creates self-debt without swap fee, enabling fee-free debt restructuring that bypasses protocol's primary fee mechanism
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same RESERVED_ID path in Compensate.executeCompensate creating self-debt at zero swap fee cost. TECH-H007 selected for most comprehensive coverage including both swap fee and fragmentation fee bypass aspects.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: swapFeeAPR has no validation at initialization, allowing misconfiguration where swapFeeAPR * maxTenor >= PERCENT * YEAR causes underflow in getCreditAmountIn
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the missing swapFeeAPR validation in Initialize.validateInitializeFeeConfigParams. TECH-H008 selected for most detailed analysis including config evidence from Deploy.sol.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: ETH deposit uses address(this).balance instead of msg.value, allowing forced ETH capture by next depositor and breaking multiple ETH deposits in multicall
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same address(this).balance override in Deposit.executeDeposit line 60-64. ECON-H018 selected for best attack economics analysis and clear exploitation path.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: variablePoolBorrowRateStaleRateInterval=0 or stale rate causes permanent revert in getAdjustedAPR for any yield curve with non-zero marketRateMultiplier, disabling variable-rate offers
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the STALE_RATE revert in YieldCurveLibrary.getAdjustedAPR. OPS-H014 selected for HIGH severity and coverage of both interval=0 and interval>0 staleness scenarios.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: NonTransferrableScaledToken.transferFrom rounding via mulDivDown on value-to-scaled conversion can accumulate dust drift across many transfers, creating accounting discrepancy
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same mulDivDown rounding in NonTransferrableScaledToken.transferFrom. OPS-H015 selected for most detailed investigation rationale and clearest preconditions.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: BuyCreditMarket for existing credit positions sets borrower=creditPosition.lender, using the wrong entity's BorrowOffer for pricing on secondary market purchases
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same borrower=creditPosition.lender assignment in BuyCreditMarket.sol line 54-88. TECH-H014 selected for HIGH severity and most comprehensive analysis of the semantic mismatch and value extraction path.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Compensate fragmentation fee capped by user's collateral balance allows fee minimization via pre-withdrawing collateral before compensating
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the Math.min(fragmentationFee, collateralToken.balanceOf) cap in Compensate.executeCompensate that enables fee minimization. ECON-H006 selected for most detailed attack economics including the multicall optimization path.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: SetUserConfiguration iterates over unbounded creditPositionIds array with no length cap, enabling gas DoS via extremely large arrays
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same unbounded for-loop in SetUserConfiguration.sol. TECH-H018 selected for more detailed investigation rationale.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Fragmentation fee dead zone in SellCreditMarket/BuyCreditMarket where cashAmount near maxCashAmount but within fragmentationFee range causes transaction revert
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same revert condition in AccountingLibrary where orders near maximum capacity fail due to fragmentation fee threshold. ECON-H016 selected for more comprehensive description.
